features.evidence: 120 Hz IPS display is gorgeous for gaming, streaming, and creative work alike. Colors are rich and vibrant, motion is smooth thanks to the high refresh rate, and the pixel density makes everything look crisp and detailed — a joy to use whether you’re in battle or browsing.

Awsome laptop runs so smooth compared to others with 16gb memory, takes a little time booting on 1st boot Storage & ExpandabilityConfigurations with SSD capacities ranging from 512 GB up to multiple terabytes mean lightning‑fast load times and plenty of space for games, projects, and media.

The performance is blazing fast and I’m always hitting above 80-120 FPS. It’s paired with an NVIDIA® GeForce RTX™ 50‑series GPU (configurations vary between RTX 5050 and RTX 5060 options) that provides smooth frame rates and solid ray‑tracing support in today’s most demanding titles.
The build feels solid without being overly heavy, and the backlit keyboard not only looks great Design & Build QualityAlienware’s design is unmistakably premium.
The battery life lasts me all day without doing gaming, It does die pretty quickly if you use it on battery mode only.

The build feels solid without being overly heavy, and the backlit keyboard not only looks great The keyboard and touchpad are responsive and comfortable for day‑to‑day tasks as well as gaming marathons.

Connectivity & Everyday UseThere’s a great selection of ports for modern peripherals, including USB‑A, multiple USB‑C with DisplayPort support, HDMI 2.1, and Ethernet — perfect for plugging into monitors, hubs, or high‑speed networks. Item arrived with a non functional Ethernet port and a semi functional USB A port.
The initial setup took me practically minutes and Windows 11 HOME immediately updated over internet. As well as several software issues.
The keyboard and touchpad are responsive and comfortable for day‑to‑day tasks as well as gaming marathons.
